name: Nix code quality, readability, and docs

description: Refresh architecture module inventory to match current Darwin and Home modules

asi.hypothesis: Updating ARCHITECTURE.md's module inventory to match actual imports will improve human navigability and remove stale references such as modules/home/skills.nix.
asi.best_practice_basis: DeepWiki/Home Manager guidance emphasizes discoverable module organization and documented options/modules; this change makes the repo's module map truthful.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not benchmark-only: every added basename is path-qualified in the module tree with a short responsibility note, and a stale skills.nix reference was replaced by claude.nix.
asi.tooling_note: Using dedicated worktree /Users/martinfan/nix-config-autoresearch after earlier hidden/global worktree paths were pruned during failed autoresearch rollback.

description: Wrap long Nix source lines for readability

asi.hypothesis: Breaking long Nix source lines into named values, string concatenations, and wrapped shell commands will improve readability without behavior changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness gate failed because tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix still needed nixpkgs-fmt after the edits; the idea itself is promising but must be reapplied with formatting before measurement.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the long-line wrapping changes, run `nix fmt` immediately, then rerun the experiment. Include the same real readability edits: split brew assertion messages, font option text, Chrome Canary env paths, fomo alias, eza alias flags, fgb preview command, Gemini jq command, Sourcegraph Amp update hint, and long test messages.
asi.learned: The metric reached quality_debt=50 with long_nix_lines=0, so the hypothesis has high potential if formatting is fixed. Do not count this as kept until checks pass.

description: Reapply long-line readability wrapping with nix fmt

asi.hypothesis: Reapplying the long-line readability edits after nix fmt should pass the format gate while keeping long_nix_lines at zero.
asi.rollback_reason: format-test still reported tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix would be reformatted, so the local `nix fmt` command did not match the check's nixpkgs-fmt invocation for that file or did not apply the needed change.
asi.next_action_hint: Before reapplying, run `nixpkgs-fmt --check tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` and `nixpkgs-fmt tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` directly; inspect the specific diff. Then reapply only after the targeted format check is clean.
asi.learned: The readability edits still reduce quality_debt to 50, but repeated format failure means the next attempt must validate the exact check command, not just `nix fmt`.

description: Wrap long Nix source lines while preserving behavior

asi.hypothesis: Named constants, string concatenation, and wrapped shell commands can eliminate nearly all long Nix source lines while making intent clearer and preserving behavior.
asi.adversarial_check: Did not touch the one remaining long BetterMouse assertion line because the first attempt showed the formatter wanted a specific shape; keeping one clear line is better than fighting the formatter for a marginal point.
asi.quality_guard: Manual `nixpkgs-fmt --check` reported 0/69 would be reformatted before run_experiment; autoresearch checks passed.
asi.changes_summary: Split long brew assertion messages, font option text, Chrome Canary paths, climode Python fallback, fomo alias, tmux copy pipe, eza alias flags, fgb preview construction, Gemini jq command, Sourcegraph Amp update hint, format-test find command, and a long mkSystem assertion message.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining quality_debt is the documented statix/deadnix contract gap. Next promising path: fix a small subset of statix warnings (empty patterns/manual inherit) or add a narrower static-lint gate only after making the checked subset clean.

description: Wire real statix/deadnix lint gate with scoped statix policy

asi.hypothesis: Fix actionable statix/deadnix findings, document a narrow statix repeated_keys opt-out, and add a real unit-static-lint check to close the documented lint-contract gap honestly.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness gate timed out after 600s while fetching/building Nix dependencies for the expanded check set, before reporting an actual test failure. The benchmark metric and local direct statix/deadnix checks were promising, but the cycle did not complete.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry after cache warm-up, or first run the new `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.unit-static-lint --no-link` directly. Keep the same edits: statix.toml disabling only repeated_keys with rationale; fix empty arg patterns in amp/crush/git/yazi; inherit cleanups in flake/tests/sourcegraph; remove unused zsh pkgs and opencode args; add unit-static-lint to tests/default.nix, README, and autoresearch.checks.sh.
asi.learned: Direct local checks before run_experiment passed for statix with `--ignore references/**` and deadnix over an explicit find-filtered Nix file list. Static-lint should avoid deadnix's fragile --exclude parsing by piping find output to xargs.
asi.timeout_detail: Checks spent the timeout fetching/building dependencies (NUR, git/perl stack, gh, etc.) after flake output changes; no failing assertion was shown.

description: Wire real statix/deadnix lint gate with scoped statix policy

asi.hypothesis: Fix actionable statix/deadnix findings, document a narrow statix repeated_keys opt-out, and add a real unit-static-lint check to close the documented lint-contract gap honestly.
asi.quality_guard: Prebuilt `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.unit-static-lint` before run_experiment to validate the new lint gate and warm the cache after the previous timeout; run_experiment checks then passed in 28.8s.
asi.statix_policy: Added statix.toml disabling only repeated_keys because this Home Manager repo intentionally keeps dotted `home.*` and `programs.*` declarations near activation comments and guards; all other statix rules remain active.
asi.changes_summary: Added unit-static-lint to tests/default.nix, tests/README.md, and autoresearch.checks.sh; fixed empty module args in amp/crush/git/yazi; cleaned inherit/manual arg warnings in flake.nix, tests/default.nix, sourcegraph-amp, zsh, opencode, and opencode-electron; deadnix scans only source files outside references via find/xargs.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not merely a metric mention: the check runs real statix and deadnix commands, excludes reference repos explicitly, and passes under the correctness gate.

description: Wrap final long assertion message for readability

asi.hypothesis: The last long test assertion message can be split into a normal Nix string concatenation, making the assertion easier to scan without changing behavior.
asi.quality_guard: Ran `nixpkgs-fmt --check tests/integration/configurations-eval-test.nix` before run_experiment; autoresearch checks passed in 27.5s.
asi.adversarial_check: This is a tiny readability edit, not benchmark cheating: it preserves the exact assertion message content and only wraps an over-140-character string in a test file.
asi.next_action_hint: Primary metric is now zero. Future iterations should improve the benchmark itself or pursue qualitative architecture/test improvements, not try to reduce the current metric further.

description: Audit saturated quality metric and refresh autoresearch notes

asi.hypothesis: With the current primary metric at its lower bound, the most honest next iteration is to verify the floor, refresh persisted session notes, and avoid fabricating a fake code improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 0, so there is no keepable improvement under the current objective. No source-code changes should be kept solely to create activity.
asi.next_action_hint: Reinitialize a broader quality metric before further optimization. Candidate signals: stale ARCHITECTURE Last reviewed date, stale documented module paths, option documentation coverage for custom modules, CI/check coverage parity, or autoresearch.md freshness. Do not make the current metric negative or remove checks to manufacture progress.
asi.learned: The current metric and checks are stable at quality_debt=0 with checks passing in 23.8s. Continued work needs a new measurement target or qualitative refactor with acceptance that the old metric cannot improve.
asi.preserved_state: Updated autoresearch.md What's Been Tried so a fresh agent sees the kept changes and the metric-floor guidance.

name: Nix documentation truthfulness and review freshness

description: Baseline broader documentation-truth metric

asi.hypothesis: The previous quality_debt metric is saturated, so reinitialize around doc_truth_debt to catch source-of-truth documentation drift without weakening existing checks.
asi.baseline_reason: New metric retains quality_debt as a secondary monitor and adds stale review/policy signals; baseline shows only ARCHITECTURE.md Last reviewed is stale under this metric.
asi.adversarial_check: This is a benchmark expansion, not a shortcut: existing checks still run, quality_debt remains emitted, and the new debt points to a concrete maintenance contract in ARCHITECTURE.md.

description: Refresh architecture review date after full doc read

asi.hypothesis: After re-reading ARCHITECTURE.md end-to-end, updating its Last reviewed date removes real documentation-maintenance debt and satisfies the document's own maintenance contract.
asi.quality_guard: Read ARCHITECTURE.md fully before changing the date; run_experiment checks passed with the expanded static-lint gate.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not just date-gaming: the doc was read end-to-end in the session, and no content contradiction was found that required additional edits before bumping the review date.
asi.baseline_context: New doc_truth_debt baseline was 10 solely from stale_review_date=1; all other truthfulness and quality secondary metrics were already zero.

name: Autoresearch prompt, belief, and experiment guidance quality

description: Baseline recursive loop-guidance metric

asi.hypothesis: Since code/docs truth metrics are saturated, the next honest objective is improving persisted autoresearch guidance so future iterations carry explicit beliefs, assumptions, search goals, hypotheses, experiment queues, and realism guardrails.
asi.user_instruction: User asked to use recursive review to improve the prompt/goal/search goal/experiment and update beliefs, hypotheses, and assumptions while staying flexible and realistic.
asi.recursive_tool_note: RLM attempts failed in this environment (missing paths/certificate errors), so this baseline codifies the recursive-review target and leaves room for available subagents/manual review until RLM is usable.
asi.adversarial_check: This does not weaken existing checks: doc_truth_debt and quality_debt remain secondary monitors at zero, and correctness checks still run.

description: Add recursive loop beliefs, assumptions, search goals, and guardrails

asi.hypothesis: Explicit persisted beliefs, assumptions to re-check, search goals, hypotheses, experiment queue, recursive-review plan, and realism guardrails will make resumed autoresearch more flexible and less likely to overfit saturated metrics.
asi.user_instruction: Directly addresses the user's request to update beliefs/hypotheses/assumptions and improve the prompt, goal, search goal, and experiment framing.
asi.quality_guard: Existing doc_truth_debt and quality_debt remained zero; autoresearch checks passed in 22s.
asi.adversarial_check: The new sections are not empty headings: each contains concrete constraints, search directions, or next experiments tied to the repo. They do not weaken checks or exclude active files.
asi.recursive_tool_note: RLM was attempted before this change but failed in this environment; the new recursive/delegated review plan records that assumption and fallback path instead of pretending success.

description: Baseline custom option documentation metric

asi.hypothesis: Custom module options have descriptions but many lack examples; tracking option examples gives a realistic next documentation-quality target after prior metrics saturated.
asi.baseline_reason: New option_doc_debt keeps all previous metrics as secondary monitors and adds option_missing_examples/option_missing_descriptions. Baseline found 39 mkOption declarations, all described, with 31 lacking examples.
asi.adversarial_check: This target aligns with Home Manager option-documentation best practice and does not weaken checks. Examples should be meaningful values users could copy, not token examples added solely to satisfy the counter.

description: Add examples to all custom mkOption declarations

asi.hypothesis: Adding realistic examples to every custom `lib.mkOption` makes module options easier to discover and use, matching Home Manager option-documentation best practices after descriptions were already complete.
asi.quality_guard: Ran nixpkgs-fmt on touched option files before run_experiment; full autoresearch checks passed in 31.1s with static lint still enabled.
asi.adversarial_check: Examples are concrete values users could copy or adapt (paths, Ghostty theme/font/window settings, Rime config dir), not dummy placeholders. No defaults or behavior changed.
asi.changes_summary: Added examples for BetterMouse profile, Rime config path, and the remaining Ghostty theme/font/cursor/window/scrollback/shell/quick-terminal mkOptions.
asi.next_action_hint: The option_doc_debt metric is now saturated. Next honest target should expand to behavioral test coverage or CI/check parity rather than adding more docs text.

name: Check parity across local recipes, CI, and autoresearch gate

description: Baseline check-parity metric with parser bug

asi.hypothesis: A check-parity metric can reveal drift between the source-of-truth flake checks, local just recipes, CI, and the focused autoresearch correctness gate.
asi.rollback_reason: The first parser for the `just check` recipe used an over-strict regex and failed to capture the recipe body, so it overcounted all test attributes as missing instead of recognizing the two checks currently listed manually.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the justfile recipe parser to match `check:` directly, reinitialize the same check-parity objective to get a valid baseline, then improve real drift by making `just check` delegate to `nix flake check` plus the active Darwin system build.
asi.adversarial_check: Discarding this run avoids keeping a misleading metric baseline even though the shell command and correctness checks passed.

name: Check parity across local recipes, CI, and autoresearch gate

description: Baseline check-parity metric

asi.hypothesis: A check-parity metric can reveal drift between the source-of-truth flake checks, local just recipes, CI, and the focused autoresearch correctness gate.
asi.baseline_reason: The valid baseline found `just check` manually lists only two of nine test attributes while CI runs `nix flake check`, the active Darwin build is present, and the autoresearch gate covers all unit/integration checks it intentionally focuses on.
asi.quality_guard: All previous saturated metrics stayed at zero; autoresearch correctness checks passed in 21.3s.
asi.adversarial_check: The metric does not penalize the focused autoresearch gate for omitting smoke builds, but it does penalize the developer-facing `just check` recipe whose comment promises the full check suite.
asi.parser_fix_note: A previous discarded run overcounted because the justfile recipe parser failed to match `check:`; this baseline uses the corrected parser and reports the two manually listed checks correctly.

description: Make just check delegate to flake check

asi.hypothesis: Replacing a stale manual `just check` subset with `nix flake check` will keep the local developer recipe aligned with the source-of-truth check attr set while still building the active Darwin system explicitly.
asi.quality_guard: Validated the just recipe with `just --dry-run check`; run_experiment checks passed in 19.9s and all previous debt metrics remained zero.
asi.adversarial_check: This is not benchmark-only: the prior justfile comment promised the full check suite but listed only two check attrs, so delegating to `nix flake check` removes a brittle duplicate list and makes future test additions automatically covered locally.
asi.changes_summary: `just check` now runs `nix flake check --show-trace --print-build-logs` and then builds `.#darwinConfigurations.f.system` with `--show-trace`. Autoresearch notes record the saturated option-doc target and this check-parity finding.
asi.next_action_hint: The check_parity_debt metric is now saturated. Next honest target should look at behavioral coverage for high-risk custom modules or a small source refactor backed by tests.

name: Behavioral integration coverage for active custom modules

description: Baseline behavioral integration coverage metric

asi.hypothesis: Behavioral integration assertions for active high-risk custom modules with activation scripts or generated config will catch regressions that documentation and check-parity metrics miss.
asi.baseline_reason: The baseline found missing assertions for Rime enablement/package/activation, Ghostty generated config/theme output, and BetterMouse seed/launchd behavior while all previous debt metrics remained zero.
asi.quality_guard: Existing autoresearch correctness checks passed in 22.6s; check_parity_debt, option_doc_debt, loop_guidance_debt, doc_truth_debt, and quality_debt all stayed at zero.
asi.adversarial_check: This metric is intentionally scoped to active high-risk modules with activation scripts or generated files, not every possible setting. The next change must add real assertions over evaluated config, not marker-only names.

description: Add behavioral assertions for Rime, Ghostty, and BetterMouse

asi.hypothesis: Adding real integration assertions for high-risk active modules will protect behavior that was previously only implied by evaluation: Rime activation scripts, Ghostty generated config/theme files, and BetterMouse seed/launchd wiring.
asi.quality_guard: Ran nixpkgs-fmt on the touched integration test and directly built `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.integration-configurations-eval` before run_experiment; full autoresearch checks then passed in 11.8s.
asi.adversarial_check: The new assertions inspect evaluated activation script text, Home Manager config files, launchd ProgramArguments, and package membership; they are not marker-only names added to satisfy the metric.
asi.changes_summary: Added 8 integration assertions: Rime enablement/package/postActivation/user activation, Ghostty generated config/custom theme file, and BetterMouse seed activation/launchd agents.
asi.next_action_hint: behavioral_coverage_debt is now saturated. Next target could be a small source refactor backed by these tests, such as factoring repeated Home Manager activation paths/log dirs, or a metric for duplicated activation literals if it reveals real simplification opportunities.

description: Baseline activation path literal metric

asi.hypothesis: Repeated high-risk activation path literals in Rime and BetterMouse modules can now be safely factored into named constants because integration tests characterize the generated activation scripts and launchd wiring.
asi.baseline_reason: The baseline found six duplicate occurrences beyond one source-of-truth each: BetterMouse Application Support, Rime user config directory, and /Library/Input Methods in activation scripts.
asi.quality_guard: All previous saturated metrics stayed at zero and autoresearch checks passed in 20.9s.
asi.adversarial_check: The metric is scoped to activation paths with real behavior risk and existing tests, not arbitrary string de-duplication. The follow-up should reduce repeated source literals by naming paths without changing evaluated behavior.

description: Name repeated Rime and BetterMouse activation paths

asi.hypothesis: Factoring repeated high-risk activation paths into named Nix constants will make Rime and BetterMouse activation scripts easier to maintain without changing generated behavior.
asi.quality_guard: Ran nixpkgs-fmt on touched files and directly built `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.integration-configurations-eval`; run_experiment checks passed in 11.6s and all previous debt metrics stayed at zero.
asi.adversarial_check: The refactor names real domain paths (`bettermouseConfigDir`, `rimeUserDir`, `inputMethodsDir`, `squirrelApp`, `packagedSquirrelApp`, `squirrelExecutable`) and is backed by behavioral assertions over the evaluated activation text and launchd args. It does not delete checks or weaken scope.
asi.changes_summary: Replaced repeated BetterMouse Application Support, Rime user config, and Squirrel input-method paths with named constants; updated the Rime activation assertion to match the equivalent generated target assignment.
asi.next_action_hint: activation_path_literal_debt is now saturated. Next target should either broaden to another real maintainability signal (e.g. generated-config tests for prompt/zsh if gaps exist) or pursue a small refactor in `modules/home/claude.nix` only after characterizing behavior with tests.

description: Baseline Claude activation coverage metric

asi.hypothesis: The large `modules/home/claude.nix` should be characterized with integration assertions for managed files and activation scripts before any future split or refactor.
asi.baseline_reason: The baseline found five missing Claude assertions: managed files, settings seed, Stop-hook debug wrapping, disabled grill skills, and superpowers brainstorming de-duplication.
asi.quality_guard: All previously saturated metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 21.8s.
asi.adversarial_check: This metric focuses on real evaluated Home Manager behavior in a large high-risk module, not arbitrary line-count reduction. The next change must inspect actual files and activation script text, not add marker-only names.

description: Add Claude activation integration assertions

asi.hypothesis: Adding real integration assertions for `modules/home/claude.nix` will characterize the large module's managed files and activation scripts before any future split or refactor.
asi.quality_guard: Ran nixpkgs-fmt on the touched integration test and directly built `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.integration-configurations-eval` before run_experiment; full autoresearch checks passed in 10.5s.
asi.adversarial_check: The new tests inspect evaluated Home Manager file declarations and activation script text for the Claude launcher/docs/hooks, settings seed, Stop-hook wrapper, disabled grill skill sweep, and plugin brainstorming de-duplication. They are not marker-only assertion names.
asi.changes_summary: Added five Darwin integration assertions covering Claude managed files, settings seed activation, Stop-hook debug wrapping, disabled grill-me cleanup, and superpowers plugin brainstorming parking.
asi.next_action_hint: claude_activation_coverage_debt is now saturated. A future iteration can use these tests to support a focused split of `modules/home/claude.nix` into smaller files, or broaden coverage to generated zsh/git/tmux config if a real gap is found.

description: Baseline Agent Skills docs truth metric

asi.hypothesis: After adding Claude activation coverage, ARCHITECTURE.md may contain stale Agent Skills mechanism claims that should be refreshed before any code split or refactor.
asi.baseline_reason: Baseline found ARCHITECTURE.md still says the Claude/Agent Skills module has no custom activation scripts, while modules/home/claude.nix now has multiple home.activation blocks covered by integration tests.
asi.quality_guard: All previous debt metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 24.8s.
asi.adversarial_check: The metric compares a source-of-truth doc claim against actual module structure; it does not reward deleting docs, only making the claim truthful.

description: Refresh Agent Skills activation docs

asi.hypothesis: Refreshing ARCHITECTURE.md's Agent Skills mechanism paragraph to acknowledge Claude-specific activation scripts will keep source-of-truth docs aligned with the now-tested Home Manager behavior.
asi.quality_guard: Run_experiment checks passed in 44.9s; all previous debt metrics stayed at zero.
asi.adversarial_check: The edit does not hide the activation scripts to appease the metric; it explicitly names their responsibilities and distinguishes them from the upstream DSL-managed skill bundle/target sync.
asi.changes_summary: Updated the Agent Skills mechanism paragraph to say `programs.agent-skills` owns the skill bundle and target sync, while adjacent Claude activations handle settings seeding, Stop-hook diagnostics, and duplicate-skill cleanup after `writeBoundary`.
asi.next_action_hint: agent_docs_truth_debt is now saturated. Next useful target could be a small tested split of `modules/home/claude.nix` or another docs-truth audit for stale root reports outside references.

description: Baseline Claude activation locality metric

asi.hypothesis: Now that Claude activation behavior is covered by integration tests and documented truthfully, moving those activation blocks into a focused submodule can improve locality without changing behavior.
asi.baseline_reason: Baseline found four Claude-specific activation blocks still embedded in `modules/home/claude.nix` and no focused `claude-common.nix` / `claude-activations.nix` support modules.
asi.quality_guard: All prior debt metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 20.2s.
asi.adversarial_check: This metric does not reward arbitrary line-count chopping; it targets the tested activation boundary and requires a shared common module to avoid duplicating target/disabled-skill constants.

description: Split Claude activation maintenance into focused module

asi.hypothesis: Moving the already-tested Claude runtime activation scripts into a focused submodule will improve locality while keeping `modules/home/claude.nix` as the orchestrator for managed files and agent-skills wiring.
asi.quality_guard: Ran nixpkgs-fmt on touched Claude modules, directly built `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.integration-configurations-eval`, and then run_experiment checks passed in 10.5s. All earlier debt metrics remained zero.
asi.adversarial_check: This is a boundary-driven split, not arbitrary line-count shaving: the moved code matches the activation behaviors already covered by integration assertions, and shared skill target/disabled-skill constants moved to `claude-common.nix` instead of being duplicated.
asi.changes_summary: Added `modules/home/claude-activations.nix` for Claude settings seed, Stop-hook diagnostics, grill-me cleanup, and superpowers brainstorming cleanup; added `modules/home/claude-common.nix` for shared skill target and selection constants; updated `modules/home/claude.nix` imports and `ARCHITECTURE.md` inventory.
asi.next_action_hint: claude_activation_locality_debt is now saturated. Next useful targets: verify root docs/reports for stale `modules/home/skills.nix` claims, or consider a similarly tested split only where behavior is already covered.

description: Baseline Agent Skills report truth metric

asi.hypothesis: After splitting Claude activation code, the root Agent Skills design report may contain stale implementation, target-structure, and verification claims that should be refreshed before future agents rely on it.
asi.baseline_reason: Baseline found five stale report claims: single live file, old modules/home/skills.nix verification, symlink-tree recommendation/target snippet, and old Windows/no-Nix verification status.
asi.quality_guard: All previous debt metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 20.0s.
asi.adversarial_check: The metric targets concrete stale statements in a human-facing report; the follow-up should update the report to match evaluated repo behavior, not delete the report or hide context.

description: Refresh Agent Skills root report

asi.hypothesis: Refreshing AGENT_SKILLS_NIX_REPORT.md after the Claude module split will keep a human-facing design report aligned with the current live modules, target structure, and verification status.
asi.quality_guard: run_experiment checks passed in 20.8s; all previous debt metrics stayed at zero.
asi.adversarial_check: The report was updated rather than deleted: it now names the split live modules, shows current `structure = "link"` target wiring, and replaces obsolete Windows/no-Nix verification notes with the checks actually run in this autoresearch environment.
asi.changes_summary: Updated the Agent Skills report's current implementation, target-structure recommendation, `link` rationale, and verification status to match `modules/home/claude.nix`, `claude-common.nix`, and `claude-activations.nix`.
asi.next_action_hint: agent_report_truth_debt is now saturated. Next useful target could audit root docs for missing mention of the new Claude split, or broaden to README-style stale path checks outside ARCHITECTURE.md and AGENT_SKILLS_NIX_REPORT.md.

description: Baseline root fallback docs metric

asi.hypothesis: A root docs-truth audit can catch stale repository-layout claims that earlier module/test metrics missed, especially claims about fallback files that no longer exist.
asi.baseline_reason: Baseline found ARCHITECTURE.md still documents top-level `skills.nix` and says both `home.nix` and `skills.nix` exist, but the worktree has no `skills.nix`.
asi.quality_guard: All previous debt metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 21.8s.
asi.adversarial_check: This target compares documented root files to actual root files. The follow-up should remove or rephrase the stale fallback claim, not create a dummy `skills.nix` just to satisfy the doc.

description: Remove stale skills.nix fallback docs

asi.hypothesis: Removing the stale top-level `skills.nix` fallback from ARCHITECTURE.md will make the repository layout and escape-hatch documentation match the actual root files.
asi.quality_guard: run_experiment checks passed in 20.6s; all previous debt metrics remained zero.
asi.adversarial_check: Did not create a dummy `skills.nix` just to satisfy documentation. The doc now says only the existing `home.nix` compatibility shim exists, and points Agent Skills wiring back to the flake/Home Manager modules.
asi.changes_summary: Updated ARCHITECTURE.md root tree and renamed the off-flake fallback section to an off-flake compatibility shim describing only `home.nix`.
asi.next_action_hint: root_fallback_docs_debt is now saturated. Next useful target could audit remaining root docs for stale path claims outside references, or broaden docs-truth checks to AGENT_SKILLS_NIX_REPORT/ARCHITECTURE consistency.

name: Architecture Agent Skills target policy truth

description: Baseline architecture Agent Skills policy metric

asi.hypothesis: After refreshing the Agent Skills report, ARCHITECTURE.md may still contain stale Agent Skills target-policy text from the earlier symlink-tree/rsync setup.
asi.baseline_reason: Baseline found three stale architecture claims: global targets should use symlink-tree, enabled targets use rsync deletion semantics, and excludePatterns should default to ["/.system"] even though the live Claude module uses static `link` targets and `excludePatterns = [ ]`.
asi.quality_guard: All previous debt metrics remained zero and autoresearch checks passed in 23.9s.
asi.adversarial_check: This metric compares source-of-truth architecture policy to actual live module behavior. The follow-up should explain the current link/static-target policy rather than weakening or removing Agent Skills docs.

description: Refresh Agent Skills target policy docs

asi.hypothesis: Updating ARCHITECTURE.md's Agent Skills target policy to match current static `link` targets and fully Nix-owned roots will remove stale operational guidance that could mislead future target changes.
asi.quality_guard: run_experiment checks passed in 24.1s; all previous debt metrics remained zero.
asi.adversarial_check: The edit keeps the operational warnings instead of deleting them: it distinguishes `link` ownership from `symlink-tree`/`copy-tree` deletion behavior and explains when `excludePatterns = [ ]` is intentional versus when `/.system` should be preserved.
asi.changes_summary: Refreshed the Agent Skills policy bullets, project-local target guidance, and Design Q&A risk answer in ARCHITECTURE.md to match current static `link` target wiring.
asi.next_action_hint: architecture_agent_policy_debt is now saturated. Next useful target could broaden docs-truth checks to HOTKEYS.md or generated configuration docs, or pause for review if further metrics look too narrow.
